mnemonics: CRAWL, INDEX, SERVE - The three core components of a search engine architecture: Crawler, Indexer, and Query Processor/Serving System.
keyPoints: A search engine's architecture is typically divided into an offline (crawling and indexing) and an online (query processing and ranking) system.
The inverted index is the fundamental data structure that allows for fast lookup of documents containing query terms.
Distributed architectures are essential for modern search engines to handle the massive scale of the web and user queries.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the indexer (offline, builds data structures) and the query processor (online, uses those structures).
Assuming a single, monolithic index exists for the entire web, rather than a distributed and partitioned system.
